meaning: adj.无脊椎的,无骨气的n.无脊椎动物,无骨气的人
examples: Most invertebrate species disappeared during a wave of extinctions.
Increasing salinity caused by the evaporation resulted in the extermination of scores of invertebrate species.
People aren't even required to seek permits to move plants or invertebrate animals around as long as they are not classified as pests.
My client and I argued that it was an invertebrate, and indeed was meat.
The matter was referred to the Press Complaints Commission, the invertebrate body that oversees the misdeeds of British newspapers.
